home_testimonials_items_item_2_quote: The same content can become a match game, a quiz, or a worksheet-style follow-up without rewriting the lesson.
home_testimonials_items_item_3_quote: The result view turns homework into a quick reteach plan instead of just another score table.
home_testimonials_items_item_4_quote: I can open a student link, watch the attempt summary, and know what to review next.
home_testimonials_items_item_5_quote: The assignment flow is specific enough for a small class: play first, submit next, review after.
home_testimonials_items_item_6_quote: The product is starting with practical templates but clearly leaves room for richer AI-assisted worksheet workflows.

roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_1_descriptio: Read teacher-uploaded worksheets into structured prompts, accepted answers, and printable follow-up modes.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_1_evidence: Uploaded worksheet files can already attach to activities as safe private source-material references.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_1_next_step: Design teacher-reviewed extraction before reading file bytes or changing the shared activity schema.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_2_descriptio: Add multi-teacher sharing, class-level result retention, and permission rules for schools and learning centers.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_2_evidence: The product currently keeps teacher-owned activities, assignment links, student attempts, and result records scoped to the signed-in owner.
roadmap_board_tasks_backlog_2_next_step: Validate school data rules, teacher seats, and permission boundaries before broadening workspace sharing.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_0_description: Teachers can create reusable structured activities, publish assignment links, and keep snapshots stable for student attempts.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_0_evidence: The current loop saves activities, publishes share links, freezes assignment snapshots, accepts public attempts, and shows teacher results.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_0_next_step: Keep distribution and result handoff reliable while retiring copied starter surfaces in small verified waves.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_1_description: Quiz, matching games, group sorting, line matching, fill-ins, listening, pair matching, and box reveal modes share one content model.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_1_evidence: The student runner uses the same runtime answer contract across quizzes, matching games, line matching, group sorting, fill-ins, listening, pair matching, and box reveal modes.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_1_next_step: Deepen each template interaction without splitting scoring, snapshots, or result review into separate data shapes.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_2_description: Teachers can generate AI-assisted activity drafts, review coverage, inspect safe source-material provenance, and edit everything before saving.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_2_evidence: AI drafts now fill the normal activity editor with teacher-reviewable questions, pairs, groups, vocabulary, notes, readiness, trust, and safety summaries.
roadmap_board_tasks_done_2_next_step: Extend from draft creation into deterministic remix assists, distractor generation, leveled variants, and worksheet extraction.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_0_descri: Make completion, scores, item misses, exports, and review filters clearer for teacher follow-up.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_0_eviden: Result pages already expose metrics, review filters, classroom briefs, reteach plans, item review summaries, student follow-up summaries, CSV export, and printable handoff paths.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_0_next_s: Keep sharpening the current review scope, copy artifacts, and offline export confidence around the same results data.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_1_descri: Tighten printable follow-up, fill-in, line matching, and listening flows around the same assignment snapshot.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_1_eviden: Worksheet-style modes and printable assignment pages already reuse frozen assignment snapshots, delivery settings, answer reveal rules, and accepted alternatives.
roadmap_board_tasks_in_progress_1_next_s: Improve worksheet preparation, audio handoff, and future extraction without creating a parallel worksheet product.
roadmap_columns_backlog_description: Next product bets once the core classroom loop stays dependable.
roadmap_columns_done_description: The teacher and student foundation that must stay reliable while copied starter surfaces are retired.
roadmap_columns_in_progress_description: Current focus: make results, worksheet delivery, and template polish more classroom-ready.

roadmap_secondary_cta_aria_label: Browse available ClassGamify templates from the roadmap page.
roadmap_snapshot_expansion_description: AI drafts now stay inside the teacher-reviewed activity model; the next expansion is remix assistance and worksheet extraction.
roadmap_snapshot_live_description: Reusable activities, assignment links, student runners, and teacher result review are the product foundation.
roadmap_snapshot_loop_description: The next passes deepen worksheet-style delivery, listening prompts, drag interactions, and reteach summaries.
roadmap_subtitle: A practical view of what is already usable, what is being tightened now, and how teacher-reviewed AI creation expands next.
roadmap_validation_description: We prioritize work that can be tested in the teacher flow, improves assignment delivery or review, and strengthens the data model instead of creating one-off demos.
roadmap_validation_item_business_descrip: Teachers should understand why a workflow saves prep, delivery, or reteach time before any paid plan boundary appears.
roadmap_validation_item_learning_descrip: A feature is stronger when the same activity can become a game, assignment, worksheet, and review artifact without losing context.
roadmap_validation_item_workflow_descrip: Classroom and learning-center workflows should handle sharing links, student names, result retention, and team permissions deliberately.
roadmap_validation_title: Every roadmap item needs classroom proof
